title: bromus commutatus subsp. scabratus
intro: Bromus commutatus subsp. scabratus, commonly known as rough meadow brome, is a subspecies of grass native to Europe and parts of Asia. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The rough meadow brome has been associated with various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Bromus commutatus subsp. scabratus include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
